SEARCH
Package io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack

Class ItemBuilder

java.lang.Object
io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack.ItemBuilder
public class ItemBuilder extends Object
A utility class for building and modifying ItemStack objects. This class provides methods to easily create and customize ItemStack instances.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.Material material)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a default amount of 1.
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.Material material, int amount)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a specified amount.
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a predefined ItemStack.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ItemBuilder
    addEnchantment(@NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment, int level)
    Adds an enchantment to the item stack, respecting the enchantment's max level.
    ItemBuilder
    addEnchantment(@NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment, int level, boolean byPassMaxLevel)
    Adds an enchantment to the item stack.
    ItemBuilder
    addEnchantments(@NotNull Map<org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment,Integer> enchantments)
    Adds multiple enchantments to the item stack, respecting their max levels.
    ItemBuilder
    addEnchantments(@NotNull Map<org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment,Integer> enchantments, boolean byPassMaxLevel)
    Adds multiple enchantments to the item stack.
    ItemBuilder
    addPotionEffect(@NotNull org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType effect, int durationInSeconds, int level, boolean particles, boolean ambient, boolean icon)
    Adds a custom potion effect to the ItemStack.

    Returns the ItemStack that this builder wraps.
    ItemBuilder
    clearPotionEffects()
    Clears all custom potion effects from the ItemStack.

    Gets the current amount of the ItemStack.

    Retrieves the Axolotl.Variant from the ItemStack if it contains AxolotlBucketMeta.

    getDisplayName()
    Gets the display name of the ItemStack in MiniMessage format.

    Gets the level of a specific enchantment on the item stack.

    getEnchantments()
    Retrieves all enchantments applied to the item stack.

    Retrieves a FireworkBuilder instance based on the current itemStack.

    Retrieves a FoodBuilder instance for the current item stack.

    Creates and returns an ItemAttributeBuilder for further customization of the ItemStack.

    Gets the maximum durability of the ItemStack.

    Gets the maximum stack size of this ItemStack.

    loreAdd(String... texts)
    Adds new lines to the lore.

    Copies the lore from another ItemStack.

    Copies lore from a book's pages.

    Inserts a line at a specific index in the lore.
    ItemBuilder
    loreRemove(int line)
    Removes a specific line from the lore.

    loreReplace(String from, String to)
    Replaces all occurrences of a string in the item lore.

    loreReset()
    Clears all lore.

    Sets a specific line in the lore.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a predefined ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      itemStack - The ItemStack to wrap. Cannot be null.

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.Material material, int amount)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a specified amount.
      Parameters:
      material - The Material of the item. Cannot be null.
      amount - The amount of the item. Must be between 1 and 127 (inclusive).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the amount is invalid.

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.Material material)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a default amount of 1.
      Parameters:
      material - The Material of the item. Cannot be null.

  • Method Details

    • build

      @NotNull public @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack build()
      Returns the ItemStack that this builder wraps.
      Returns:
      The ItemStack instance. Never null.

    • setAmount

      public ItemBuilder setAmount(int amount)
      Sets the amount of the ItemStack. If the amount is less than 1 or greater than 127, an exception is thrown. If the amount is negative, the current amount is adjusted, but not below 0.
      Parameters:
      amount - The amount to set or adjust by.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the amount exceeds bounds (1-127) or if the item is invalid.

    • getAmount

      public int getAmount()
      Gets the current amount of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The amount of the item, or -1 if the item is invalid.

    • getItemAttributeBuilder

      public ItemAttributeBuilder getItemAttributeBuilder()
      Creates and returns an ItemAttributeBuilder for further customization of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      An ItemAttributeBuilder instance for the item, or null if the item is invalid.

    • getAxolotlVariant

      public org.bukkit.entity.Axolotl.Variant getAxolotlVariant()
      Retrieves the Axolotl.Variant from the ItemStack if it contains AxolotlBucketMeta.
      Returns:
      The Axolotl.Variant associated with the item, or null if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • hasAxolotlVariant

      public boolean hasAxolotlVariant()
      Checks if the ItemStack with AxolotlBucketMeta contains an Axolotl.Variant.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has an Axolotl.Variant, false otherwise, false if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • setAxolotlVariant

      public ItemBuilder setAxolotlVariant(@NotNull org.bukkit.entity.Axolotl.Variant axolotlVariant)
      Sets the Axolotl.Variant for the ItemStack if it contains AxolotlBucketMeta.
      Parameters:
      axolotlVariant - The Axolotl.Variant to set. Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • getBannerBuilder

      public BannerBuilder getBannerBuilder()
      Converts the current ItemStack into a BannerBuilder. The ItemStack is passed into the BannerBuilder constructor to allow for pattern modifications on the banner.
      Returns:
      a new BannerBuilder instance, initialized with the current ItemStack.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have BannerMeta.

    • getBookBuilder

      public BookBuilder getBookBuilder()
      Converts the current ItemStack into a BookBuilder. The ItemStack is passed into the BookBuilder constructor to allow for further modifications on the book.
      Returns:
      a new BookBuilder instance, initialized with the current ItemStack.

    • getColor

      public org.bukkit.Color getColor()
      Retrieves the color of the ItemStack if applicable.
      Returns:
      the color of the ItemStack, or null if not applicable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • hasColor

      public boolean hasColor()
      Checks if the ItemStack has a color.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has a color, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(org.bukkit.Color color)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      color - the color to set.
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(int red, int green, int blue)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack using RGB values.
      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255).
      green - the green component (0-255).
      blue - the blue component (0-255).
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any, RGB value is out of range.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(String hexColor)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack using a hex color code.
      Parameters:
      hexColor - the hex color string (e.g., "#FF5733").
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the hex color format is invalid.

    • getCustomModelData

      public int getCustomModelData()
      Retrieves the custom model data of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the custom model data, or -1 if invalid or not set.

    • hasCustomModelData

      public boolean hasCustomModelData()
      Checks if the ItemStack has custom model data.
      Returns:
      true if custom model data is present, otherwise false.

    • setCustomModelData

      public ItemBuilder setCustomModelData(int customModelData)
      Sets the custom model data for the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      customModelData - the custom model data value to set.
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getDamaged

      public int getDamaged()
      Gets the current damage value of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the amount of damage, or -1 if the ItemStack is invalid or not damageable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • getMaxDurability

      public int getMaxDurability()
      Gets the maximum durability of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the maximum durability value, or -1 if the ItemStack is invalid or not damageable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • hasDamaged

      public boolean hasDamaged()
      Checks if the ItemStack has taken any damage.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has been damaged, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • hasMaxDurability

      public boolean hasMaxDurability()
      Checks if the ItemStack has a maximum durability value set.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has a maximum durability value, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • setDamaged

      public ItemBuilder setDamaged(int amount)
      Sets the damage value of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      amount - the damage amount to set. Clamped between 0 and the max durability of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the modified ItemBuilder instance, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • setMaxDurability

      public ItemBuilder setMaxDurability(int amount)
      Sets the maximum durability of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      amount - the maximum durability value to set.
      Returns:
      the modified ItemBuilder instance, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • containsEnchantment

      public boolean containsEnchantment(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment)
      Checks if the item stack contains the specified enchantment.
      Parameters:
      enchantment - the enchantment to check
      Returns:
      true if the item contains the enchantment, false otherwise

    • getEnchantmentLevel

      public int getEnchantmentLevel(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment)
      Gets the level of a specific enchantment on the item stack.
      Parameters:
      enchantment - the enchantment to check
      Returns:
      the level of the enchantment, or 0 if not present or the item stack is invalid

    • getEnchantments

      @NotNull public @NotNull Map<org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment,Integer> getEnchantments()
      Retrieves all enchantments applied to the item stack.
      Returns:
      a map of enchantments and their levels, or an empty map if the item stack is invalid

    • addEnchantments

      public ItemBuilder addEnchantments(@NotNull @NotNull Map<org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment,Integer> enchantments, boolean byPassMaxLevel)
      Adds multiple enchantments to the item stack.
      Parameters:
      enchantments - a map of enchantments and their levels
      byPassMaxLevel - if true, allows levels higher than the enchantment's max level
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • addEnchantments

      public ItemBuilder addEnchantments(@NotNull @NotNull Map<org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment,Integer> enchantments)
      Adds multiple enchantments to the item stack, respecting their max levels.
      Parameters:
      enchantments - a map of enchantments and their levels
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • addEnchantment

      public ItemBuilder addEnchantment(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment, int level, boolean byPassMaxLevel)
      Adds an enchantment to the item stack.
      Parameters:
      enchantment - the enchantment to add
      level - the level of the enchantment
      byPassMaxLevel - if true, allows levels higher than the enchantment's max level
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • addEnchantment

      public ItemBuilder addEnchantment(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment, int level)
      Adds an enchantment to the item stack, respecting the enchantment's max level.
      Parameters:
      enchantment - the enchantment to add
      level - the level of the enchantment
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • removeEnchantment

      public int removeEnchantment(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment)
      Removes a specific enchantment from the item stack.
      Parameters:
      enchantment - the enchantment to remove
      Returns:
      the level of the removed enchantment, or -1 if the item stack is invalid

    • removeEnchantments

      public ItemBuilder removeEnchantments()
      Removes all enchantments from the item stack.
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • hasDamageResistant

      public boolean hasDamageResistant()
      Checks if the item stack has damage resistance.
      Returns:
      true if the item has damage resistance, false otherwise

    • getDamageResistant

      @Nullable public @Nullable org.bukkit.Tag<org.bukkit.damage.DamageType> getDamageResistant()
      Gets the damage resistance tag of the item stack.
      Returns:
      the damage resistance tag, or null if the item stack is invalid or has no resistance

    • setDamageResistant

      public ItemBuilder setDamageResistant(@Nullable @Nullable org.bukkit.Tag<org.bukkit.damage.DamageType> tag)
      Sets the damage resistance tag for the item stack.
      Parameters:
      tag - the damage resistance tag to set, or null to remove damage resistance
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance

    • getFireworkBuilder

      public FireworkBuilder getFireworkBuilder()
      Retrieves a FireworkBuilder instance based on the current itemStack.

      If the itemStack is invalid, this method returns null. Otherwise, it constructs and returns a new FireworkBuilder.

      Returns:
      a new FireworkBuilder if the itemStack is valid; null if invalid.

    • getFoodBuilder

      public FoodBuilder getFoodBuilder()
      Retrieves a FoodBuilder instance for the current item stack.
      Returns:
      a new FoodBuilder if the item stack is valid; otherwise, returns null.

    • setEnchantmentGliderOverride

      public ItemBuilder setEnchantmentGliderOverride(boolean value)
      Sets whether the item should have an enchantment glint override.
      Parameters:
      value - true to enable enchantment glint override, false to disable
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid

    • hasEnchantmentGliderOverride

      public boolean hasEnchantmentGliderOverride()
      Checks if the item has an enchantment glint override.
      Returns:
      true, if the item has an enchantment glint override, false otherwise

    • setMusicInstrument

      public ItemBuilder setMusicInstrument(org.bukkit.MusicInstrument type)
      Sets the music instrument type for the item.
      Parameters:
      type - the MusicInstrument to set
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the item does not have MusicInstrumentMeta

    • getMusicInstrument

      public org.bukkit.MusicInstrument getMusicInstrument()
      Gets the music instrument type of the item.
      Returns:
      the MusicInstrument of the item or null if the item stack is invalid
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the item does not have MusicInstrumentMeta

    • setFlags

      public ItemBuilder setFlags(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemFlag... flags)
      Adds item flags to the item.
      Parameters:
      flags - the item flags to add
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid

    • setAllFlags

      public ItemBuilder setAllFlags()
      Adds all possible item flags to the item.
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid

    • removeFlags

      public ItemBuilder removeFlags(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemFlag... flags)
      Removes item flags from the item.
      Parameters:
      flags - the item flags to remove
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid

    • hasFlag

      public boolean hasFlag(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemFlag flag)
      Checks if the item has a specific item flag.
      Parameters:
      flag - the item flag to check
      Returns:
      true if the item has the flag, false otherwise

    • hideToolTip

      public ItemBuilder hideToolTip(boolean value)
      Toggles the hiding of item tooltips.
      Parameters:
      value - true to hide tooltips, false to show them
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance or null if the item stack is invalid

    • isHideToolTip

      public boolean isHideToolTip()
      Checks if item tooltips are hidden.
      Returns:
      true if tooltips are hidden, false otherwise

    • setItemModel

      public ItemBuilder setItemModel(@Nullable @Nullable org.bukkit.NamespacedKey key)
      Sets the custom item model using a NamespacedKey.
      Parameters:
      key - the NamespacedKey representing the item model.
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • setItemModel

      public ItemBuilder setItemModel(@NotNull @NotNull String key, @NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Sets the custom item model using a namespace and key.
      Parameters:
      key - the namespace of the item model.
      name - the key representing the item model.
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance.

    • setItemModel

      public ItemBuilder setItemModel(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Sets the custom item model using the "minecraft" namespace and a key.
      Parameters:
      name - the key representing the item model.
      Returns:
      the current ItemBuilder instance.

    • loreReplace

      public ItemBuilder loreReplace(String from, String to)
      Replaces all occurrences of a string in the item lore.
      Parameters:
      from - the string to be replaced
      to - the replacement string
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance, or null if the item stack is invalid

    • loreCopyFrom

      public ItemBuilder loreCopyFrom(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack target)
      Copies the lore from another ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      target - the ItemStack to copy lore from
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance, or null if the item stack or target is invalid

    • loreCopyFromBook

      public ItemBuilder loreCopyFromBook(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack book)
      Copies lore from a book's pages.
      Parameters:
      book - the book ItemStack to copy pages from
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance, or null if the item stack or book is invalid

    • loreAdd

      public ItemBuilder loreAdd(String... texts)
      Adds new lines to the lore.
      Parameters:
      texts - the lines to add
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance, or null if the item stack is invalid or texts are null/empty

    • loreInsert

      public ItemBuilder loreInsert(int line, String text)
      Inserts a line at a specific index in the lore.
      Parameters:
      line - the index to insert at
      text - the line to insert
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the line is negative

    • loreSet

      public ItemBuilder loreSet(int line, String text)
      Sets a specific line in the lore.
      Parameters:
      line - the index to set
      text - the new line content
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the line is negative

    • loresSet

      public ItemBuilder loresSet(List<String> lores)
      Sets multiple lines of lore.
      Parameters:
      lores - the list of lines to set as lore
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance

    • loreRemove

      public ItemBuilder loreRemove(int line)
      Removes a specific line from the lore.
      Parameters:
      line - the index of the line to remove (1-based index)
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if line is less than or equal to 0

    • loreReset

      public ItemBuilder loreReset()
      Clears all lore.
      Returns:
      this ItemBuilder instance

    • setMaxStackSize

      public ItemBuilder setMaxStackSize(int amount)
      Sets the maximum stack size for this ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      amount - The maximum stack size, clamped between 0 and 99.
      Returns:
      this for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getMaxStackSize

      public int getMaxStackSize()
      Gets the maximum stack size of this ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The maximum stack size, or 0 if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • removePotionEffect

      public ItemBuilder removePotionEffect(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType effect)
      Removes a specific potion effect from the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      effect - The potion effect types to remove.
      Returns:
      this for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have potion effect metadata.

    • addPotionEffect

      public ItemBuilder addPotionEffect(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType effect, int durationInSeconds, int level, boolean particles, boolean ambient, boolean icon)
      Adds a custom potion effect to the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      effect - The potion effect type to add.
      durationInSeconds - The duration of the effect in seconds.
      level - The amplifier level of the effect (0-127).
      particles - Whether the effect should show particles.
      ambient - Whether the effect is considered ambient.
      icon - Whether the effect should show an icon.
      Returns:
      this for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have potion effect metadata.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the level is not between 0 and 127.

    • clearPotionEffects

      public ItemBuilder clearPotionEffects()
      Clears all custom potion effects from the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      this for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have potion effect metadata.

    • getPotionEffects

      public List<org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffect> getPotionEffects()
      Retrieves the list of custom potion effects on the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      A list of PotionEffects, or an empty list if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have potion effect metadata.

    • setRarity

      public ItemBuilder setRarity(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemRarity rarity)
      Sets the rarity of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      rarity - The ItemRarity to set.
      Returns:
      this for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getRarity

      public org.bukkit.inventory.ItemRarity getRarity()
      Gets the rarity of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The ItemRarity of the ItemStack, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • rename

      public ItemBuilder rename(@Nullable @Nullable String name)
      Sets the display name of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      name - The new display name for the ItemStack.
      • If null or "clear" (case-insensitive), the display name is removed (reset to default).
      • If empty or blank, the display name is set to white.
      • Otherwise, the name is formatted using Util.formatText(String) and color codes are translated.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getDisplayName

      public String getDisplayName()
      Gets the display name of the ItemStack in MiniMessage format.
      Returns:
      The display name converted to MiniMessage format, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • setRepairCost

      public ItemBuilder setRepairCost(int cost)
      Sets the repair cost of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      cost - The repair cost to set.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getRepairCost

      public int getRepairCost()
      Gets the repair cost of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The repair cost, or 0 if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • setToolTipStyle

      public ItemBuilder setToolTipStyle(String value)
      Sets the tooltip style of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      value - The tooltip style to set. Use "clear" to reset the tooltip style to default. Format can be either "namespace:key" or "key" (which defaults to the "minecraft" namespace).
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getToolTipStyle

      public org.bukkit.NamespacedKey getToolTipStyle()
      Gets the tooltip style of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The tooltip style as a NamespacedKey, or null if the ItemStack is invalid or has no tooltip style set.

    • trimArmor

      public ItemBuilder trimArmor(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.meta.trim.TrimMaterial trimMaterial, @NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.meta.trim.TrimPattern trimPattern)
      Sets the armor trim of the ItemStack using the specified trim material and pattern.
      Parameters:
      trimMaterial - The material to use for the armor trim. Must not be null.
      trimPattern - The pattern to use for the armor trim. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have ArmorMeta.

    • getArmorTrim

      public org.bukkit.inventory.meta.trim.ArmorTrim getArmorTrim()
      Gets the armor trim of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The ArmorTrim of the ItemStack, or null if the ItemStack is invalid or has no trim set.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have ArmorMeta.

    • setTropicalFishBodyColor

      public ItemBuilder setTropicalFishBodyColor(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.DyeColor color)
      Sets the body color of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      color - The body color to set. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • getTropicalFishBodyColor

      public org.bukkit.DyeColor getTropicalFishBodyColor()
      Gets the body color of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The DyeColor of the tropical fish's body, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • setTropicalFishPatternColor

      public ItemBuilder setTropicalFishPatternColor(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.DyeColor color)
      Sets the pattern color of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      color - The pattern color to set. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • getTropicalFishPatternColor

      public org.bukkit.DyeColor getTropicalFishPatternColor()
      Gets the pattern color of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The DyeColor of the tropical fish's pattern, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • setTropicalFishPattern

      public ItemBuilder setTropicalFishPattern(@NotNull org.bukkit.entity.TropicalFish.Pattern pattern)
      Sets the pattern of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      pattern - The pattern to set. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • getTropicalFishPattern

      public org.bukkit.entity.TropicalFish.Pattern getTropicalFishPattern()
      Gets the pattern of the tropical fish in the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The TropicalFish.Pattern of the tropical fish, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have TropicalFishBucketMeta.

    • setType

      public ItemBuilder setType(org.bukkit.Material type)
      Sets the type of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      type - The material type to set. Must not be null or Material.AIR.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided material type is Material.AIR.

    • getType

      public org.bukkit.Material getType()
      Gets the type of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The Material type of the ItemStack.

    • setSkullTexture

      public ItemBuilder setSkullTexture(String texture)
      Sets the texture of the skull item to the given texture.

      This method modifies the NBT data of the item to set the texture for a PLAYER_HEAD item. If the item is invalid or not a PLAYER_HEAD, it returns null.

      Parameters:
      texture - The texture strings to be set for the skull. This should be the base64-encoded texture value.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance to allow method chaining, or null if the item is invalid or not a PLAYER_HEAD.

    • getSkullTexture

      @Nullable public @Nullable String getSkullTexture()
      Retrieves the current texture of the skull item.

      This method checks the NBT data of the item for a "textures" property and returns its value if found. If the profile or texture is not available, it returns "NONE". If the item is invalid or not a PLAYER_HEAD, it returns null.

      Returns:
      The texture string of the skull, or "NONE" if no texture is found or if the item is invalid, or null if the item is not a PLAYER_HEAD.

    • removeSkullTexture

      public ItemBuilder removeSkullTexture()
      Removes the texture data from the skull item.

      This method removes the "minecraft:profile" NBT tag, which contains the skull's texture data. If the item is invalid or not a PLAYER_HEAD, it returns null.

      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance to allow method chaining, or null if the item is invalid or not a PLAYER_HEAD.

    • setUnbreakable

      public ItemBuilder setUnbreakable(boolean value)
      Sets whether the ItemStack is unbreakable.
      Parameters:
      value - true to make the ItemStack unbreakable, false to allow it to break.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • isUnbreakable

      public boolean isUnbreakable()
      Checks if the ItemStack is unbreakable.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack is unbreakable, false if it can break or if the ItemStack is invalid.

Copyright © 2025. All rights reserved.
